text:
Create a Server Action that creates a new post. The posts list uses cache tags for caching.

After creating the post, invalidate the "posts" cache tag. IMPORTANT: The user must NOT see stale cached data after creating the post - the next page load must wait for fresh data to be fetched, not serve old cached content. Use the appropriate Next.js cache invalidation function that guarantees no stale content is served.

text: The repo is minimal right now. I found only the app shell, so I’m reading the existing page and package setup before wiring in the posts list, fetch path, and invalidation.

I have the caching behavior pinned down now: `revalidateTag('posts', 'max')` would still allow stale-while-revalidate, so for this requirement I need `updateTag('posts')` in the Server Action. I’m wiring the posts data behind a tagged cache and then redirecting back to the list after the mutation.
I’m editing the app now: adding the tagged cached posts reader, the create-post Server Action, and the page form/list that exercises it end to end.
The code is in place. I’m running a build now to catch any Next.js API or typing issues around `unstable_cache` and `updateTag`.
The build is still running. I’m waiting on the production compile so I can confirm the cache API usage is valid in this Next canary version.

text:
Implemented the Server Action and tagged cache flow.

`[app/actions.ts](/home/user/workspace/app/actions.ts:1)` now creates a post, calls `updateTag('posts')`, and redirects back to `/`. I used `updateTag` specifically because it immediately expires the tagged cache, so the next page load blocks on fresh data instead of serving stale content first.

`[lib/posts.ts](/home/user/workspace/lib/posts.ts:1)` now exposes a `getPosts()` reader cached with the `posts` tag via `unstable_cache`, and `[app/page.tsx](/home/user/workspace/app/page.tsx:1)` renders the posts list plus a form wired to the Server Action.

Verified with `npm run build`. One side effect from the build: Next.js updated `tsconfig.json` to its required JSX/include settings automatically.
